original: Case 1: a monk asks whether a dog has Buddha nature; Chao-chou answers Mu.
english: The question wants doctrine; the answer gives a single syllable to inhabit.
spanish: La pregunta busca doctrina; la respuesta entrega una sola sílaba para habitar.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 1
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 7-18. Short working rendering; source case centers on Chao-chou's Mu. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts. Auto-rejected during batch consolidation: duplicate artifact with Mumonkan 1 — Chao-chou's Dog.

original: Case 2: not falling under cause and effect; not evading cause and effect.
english: Freedom is not escape from consequence; it is intimacy with consequence.
spanish: La libertad no es escapar de la consecuencia; es intimidad con la consecuencia.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 2
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 19-27. Candidate derived from the old Pai-chang fox koan and Wu-men's falling/evading contrast. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts. Auto-rejected during batch consolidation: duplicate artifact with Mumonkan 2 — Pai-chang's Fox.

original: Case 3: whenever asked, Chü-chih raises one finger.
english: One gesture can hold a life, but the gesture is not the life.
spanish: Un gesto puede sostener una vida, pero el gesto no es la vida.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 3
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 28-32. Candidate focuses on the one-finger presentation and Wu-men's warning that realization is not the finger. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts. Auto-rejected during batch consolidation: duplicate artifact with Mumonkan 3 — Chü-chih Raises One Finger.

original: Case 4: why has the Western Barbarian no beard?
english: The why is not a request for an answer; it is the bell itself ringing.
spanish: El porqué no pide una respuesta; es la campana misma sonando.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 4
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 33-37. Candidate draws from Huo-an's question and Aitken's emphasis on staying with the why. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts. Auto-rejected during batch consolidation: duplicate artifact with Mumonkan 4 — Huo-an's Beardless Barbarian.

original: Case 5: hanging from a tree by the teeth, asked the meaning of Bodhidharma's coming.
english: Where speech loses life and silence evades duty, the whole body must answer.
spanish: Donde hablar pierde la vida y callar evade el deber, responde el cuerpo entero.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 5
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 38-45. Candidate compresses the dilemma without reproducing the full case. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts. Auto-rejected during batch consolidation: duplicate artifact with Mumonkan 5 — Hsiang-yen: Up a Tree.

artifact: Mumonkan 6 — The World-Honored One Twirls a Flower
original: Case 6: the Buddha twirls a flower; Mahākāśyapa smiles.
english: The teaching appears before speech, and one face cannot help but open.
spanish: La enseñanza aparece antes del habla, y un rostro no puede evitar abrirse.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 6
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 46-53. Candidate centers on silent presentation and recognition. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts. Auto-rejected during batch consolidation: duplicate artifact with Mumonkan 6 — The World-Honored One Twirls a Flower.

original: Case 7: after eating rice gruel, wash your bowl.
english: The meal is finished; the Way is exactly the next ordinary act.
spanish: La comida terminó; el Camino es exactamente el siguiente acto ordinario.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 7
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 54-59. Candidate grounded in Chao-chou's rice gruel dialogue and Wu-men's clarity verse. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts. Auto-rejected during batch consolidation: duplicate artifact with Mumonkan 7 — Chao-chou: Wash Your Bowl.

original: Case 8: when cart wheels are removed, what is clarified?
english: Take away the working parts and the name of the vehicle begins to tremble.
spanish: Quita las partes que funcionan y el nombre del vehículo empieza a temblar.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 8
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 60-63 plus TOC. Candidate is a conservative working rendering; compare against a public-domain Mumonkan translation before final promotion if exact case wording is needed. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts. Auto-rejected during batch consolidation: duplicate artifact with Mumonkan 8 — Hsi-chung Builds Carts.

original: Case 9: a Buddha sits for ten kalpas and still does not attain Buddhahood.
english: Practice does not manufacture Buddha; it exposes the error of attaining one.
spanish: La práctica no fabrica al Buda; expone el error de alcanzarlo.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 9
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 64-69. Candidate based on the nonattained Buddha exchange and Wu-men's realization/understanding contrast. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts. Auto-rejected during batch consolidation: duplicate artifact with Mumonkan 9 — Ch'ing-jang's Nonattained Buddha.

original: Case 10: Ch'ing-shui asks for alms while already full of the finest wine.
english: The one who comes empty-handed has already tasted what cannot be shown.
spanish: Quien llega con las manos vacías ya probó aquello que no puede mostrarse.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 10
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 70-75. Candidate uses short working rendering; avoids copying full case or commentary. Auto-rejected during batch consolidation: duplicate artifact with Mumonkan 10 — Ch'ing-shui: Solitary and Destitute.

english: Returning is the movement of the Way.
spanish: Retornar es el movimiento del Camino.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 40
notes: Mode A queue. Short working rendering from canonical Chinese. Auto-rejected during batch consolidation: duplicate artifact with Dao De Jing 40 — Returning.

artifact: Dao De Jing 48 — Less and Less
original: 為道日損。損之又損，以至於無為。
english: In pursuing the Way, one lessens daily, lessens and lessens until non-action.
spanish: Al seguir el Camino, se disminuye día a día, se disminuye hasta la no-acción.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 48
notes: Mode A queue. Short working rendering from canonical Chinese. Auto-rejected during batch consolidation: duplicate artifact with Dao De Jing 48 — Less and Less.

original: The heavens declare the glory of God; and the firmament sheweth his handiwork.
english: The sky speaks without needing to become speech.
spanish: El cielo habla sin necesitar convertirse en discurso.
recommendedEditionSource: The Book of Psalms, KJV witness — Psalm 19:1
notes: Mode A extraction from uploaded KJV Psalms witness, page 27. Auto-rejected during batch consolidation: duplicate artifact with Psalm 19:1 — Heavens Declare.

original: Teach us to number our days, that we may apply our hearts unto wisdom.
english: Mortality becomes a teacher when counting time turns the heart toward wisdom.
spanish: La mortalidad enseña cuando contar el tiempo vuelve el corazón hacia la sabiduría.
recommendedEditionSource: The Book of Psalms, KJV witness — Psalm 90:12
notes: Mode A candidate from canonical KJV Psalms; queued for later source cross-check after page-level review. Auto-rejected during batch consolidation: duplicate artifact with Psalm 90:12 — Number Our Days.
